text: My go-to for the kale caesar. Consistently great. The staff always remembers regulars and the ingredients are obviously fresh. Perfect spot before heading into the Short North for errands.
Best plant-forward lunch in Columbus. The grain bowls are incredible. Parking on N High can be rough on weekends but worth it.
Great menu but it gets busy on Saturday afternoons. Worth the wait though. The lemonade is underrated.
The shawarma bowl is everything. Rachel introduced me to this place and now it is a Thursday night staple when we do Short North. The tahini drizzle is chef's kiss.
Fast, fresh, and affordable for Short North. The falafel wrap is incredible. Always packed at lunch which tells you everything.
Quick and delicious. Love the bowls. Only knocked one star because seating is very limited. Get there early or plan to eat on the go.

The margherita here is legitimately one of the best pizzas I have had outside of Italy. Thin crust, perfect char, great quality ingredients. Brought Rachel and Chloe here last month and everyone agreed - best pizza near Westerville.
Hidden gem in Westerville. The wood-fired crust is exceptional. Cozy atmosphere perfect for a dinner with friends.
Really solid pizza. The local craft beer selection pairs well. Can get loud when busy but the food makes it worth it.

Finally a Korean spot in Columbus that does bibimbap right. The banchan spread is generous and the soft tofu soup warms you up on cold days. Priya and I discovered this for Tuesday lunches near the office.
Authentic Korean flavors in Short North! The japchae is fantastic and portion sizes are generous. Great lunch spot.
Really good food. Service is a bit slow at peak lunch but they are clearly working hard. The kimchi pancake is a must-order.
Solid Thai on N High. The pad see ew is excellent and spice levels are honest - when they say hot, they mean it. Good value for the area.
Love this place for a quick lunch. The green curry is excellent. Cash and card accepted.
Great brunch spot in German Village. The avocado toast is a bit predictable but they do it well. The coffee is strong and the neighborhood vibe is perfect for weekend catch-ups.
My favorite brunch spot. The french toast is decadent and the cocktails are strong. Perfect for slow Sunday mornings in German Village.
The bun bo hue is outstanding. Far better than the pho which is also excellent. Tiny spot but incredibly authentic. Affordable and filling for lunch.
Really good Vietnamese. The fresh spring rolls are light and flavorful. Gets busy fast so go early.
The pancake burrito is absolutely legendary. Every brunch in German Village starts here. Long waits on weekends but the food is 100% worth it.
Charming German Village spot. Very unique menu. The street corn hash is inventive and delicious. Crowded but the staff hustles.
Legitimately excellent ramen in Columbus. The tonkotsu broth is rich and the chashu is tender. Way better than I expected. Found this as a rainy-night option and it delivered.
Best ramen in Columbus by a mile. The shoyu broth is incredible. Perfect for cold Ohio evenings.
Cozy German Village brunch. The eggs benedict has a nice twist - really enjoyable. Lines move faster than you would expect given the crowd.
Great specialty coffee in Clintonville. The flat white is properly made - strong and smooth. Good place to get some remote work done on Friday mornings.
Perfect wine bar for Thursday evenings in German Village. The staff know their list and make good recommendations. The charcuterie is excellent. A regular stop for us.
